hypothesis: When extending the Bayesian optimization framework to branched and cross-linked polyamine architectures, graph-based molecular representations derived from polymer-specific pre-trained graph neural networks will demonstrate significantly greater advantage over sequence-based language model embeddings compared to their relative performance on linear polymers, because the topological connectivity information critical for predicting CO2 adsorption in non-linear architectures is fundamentally better captured by graph-structured encodings than by sequential SMILES/PSMILES tokenization schemes.
